Title: Identification of novel Nrf2 activators from Cinnamomum chartophyllum H.W. Li and their potential application of preventing oxidative insults in human lung epithelial cells

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Kelch-like ECH-associated protein; BTB, IVR, Kelch/DGR domains
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Protein level unchanged under NLD and THD treatment in this study
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: No obvious change in protein abundance after NLD or THD treatment
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NFE2L2; Cul3; Leu365; Ser508; Leu557 interaction site residues for THD docking
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: THD treatment; NLD treatment; sodium arsenite context
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: THD potentially interrupts KEAP1-Nrf2 protein-protein interaction without changing KEAP1 protein abundance, thereby favoring Nrf2 activation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KEAP1 protein levels were not altered by NLD or THD. 2. Docking suggested THD may bind the KEAP1 Kelch domain through hydrogen bonds with Leu365, Ser508, and Leu557. 3. THD therefore may activate Nrf2 by disrupting KEAP1-Nrf2 interaction rather than by lowering KEAP1 expression.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for small-molecule modulation of antioxidant defense via disruption of KEAP1-Nrf2 binding
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core common gene because mechanistic experiments directly assessed KEAP1 protein level and THD docking to KEAP1; however it is not a nutrient-metabolism gene in the classical plant sense.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces quinones to less reactive hydroquinones using NAD(P)H, supporting cellular detoxification and antioxidant defense.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phase II detoxification / antioxidant response pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supports redox detoxification downstream of the target protective metabolite/system
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protection against oxidative insult and toxicant-induced damage
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced after NLD and THD treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Dose- and time-dependently increased in MDA-MB-231 and Beas-2B cells after NLD and THD treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NLD treatment; THD treatment; sodium arsenite-induced oxidative stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: immunoblot analysis; QR assay context; dose-response and time-course treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NLD and THD increased NQO1 protein expression as a downstream antioxidant enzyme of Nrf2 signaling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NQO1 protein was dose-dependently induced by NLD and THD. 2. NQO1 was also increased over time in Beas-2B cells after treatment. 3. Its induction supported activation of the Nrf2-mediated cytoprotective program.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential biomarker/output gene for screening Nrf2 activators or engineering enhanced cellular detoxification capacity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In this paper NQO1 is a downstream responder and readout of Nrf2 activation, not the primary experimental regulator.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the ATP-dependent formation of γ-glutamylcysteine, the first and rate-limiting step of glutathione biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies immediate precursor pool for glutathione production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS scavenging and protection against oxidative and toxicant stress
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ATP-dependent amide ligase; glutamate-cysteine ligase complex
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced after NLD and THD treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Dose- and time-dependently increased in MDA-MB-231 and Beas-2B cells after NLD and THD treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NLD treatment; THD treatment; sodium arsenite-induced oxidative stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: immunoblot analysis; GSH assay; dose-response and time-course treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NLD and THD increased γ-GCS protein expression and elevated intracellular GSH levels
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. γ-GCS protein was induced by NLD and THD in a dose- and time-dependent manner. 2. Increased γ-GCS expression was associated with higher intracellular GSH. 3. Upregulation of γ-GCS likely contributed to protection from As(III)-induced oxidative damage.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to enhance glutathione-based antioxidant capacity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper reports γ-GCS at the protein level without clearly distinguishing catalytic and modifier subunits; represented here as GCLC/GCLM (γ-GCS) to reflect the reported terminology.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Protein level increased after NLD and THD treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Dose- and time-dependent protein induction in MDA-MB-231 and Beas-2B cells; nuclear accumulation after treatment
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in KEAP1-Nrf2 regulatory complex; KEAP1 homodimer described as upstream repressor complex
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: antioxidant defense and phase II detoxification gene induction; glutathione synthesis support
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation following stabilization and nuclear translocation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central cellular defense regulator and primary target of the study; activation was decisive for cytoprotection against arsenite-induced oxidative injury
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protection against oxidative insults and arsenite-induced cytotoxicity
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NLD treatment; THD treatment; sodium arsenite challenge
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ARE-luciferase reporter assay; immunoblot analysis; immunofluorescence nuclear translocation assay; protein half-life measurement; GSH assay; ROS assay; apoptosis/cell viability assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NLD and THD activated Nrf2, increased its protein stability and nuclear translocation, induced cytoprotective target genes, elevated GSH, lowered ROS, and protected cells from As(III)-induced death
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NLD and THD dose-dependently increased ARE-luciferase activity and Nrf2 protein abundance. 2. They promoted Nrf2 nuclear translocation and extended Nrf2 half-life from about 14.7 min to 25.8 min and 31.7 min, respectively. 3. Activated Nrf2 increased downstream NQO1 and γ-GCS and improved resistance to arsenite-induced oxidative stress.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No evident toxicity at Nrf2-inducing doses in the tested cell lines
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential pharmacological target for development of antioxidant-protective agents against lung oxidative injury; useful screening node for Nrf2 activators
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a mammalian cell pharmacology paper; Nrf2 is the central core gene, while the study primarily identifies small-molecule activators rather than genetic variants.
